headline: AI's reshaping South Florida's job market — entry-level roles are taking a hit while skilled tech positions boom

stories.title: Stanford economists reveal AI job reality: Entry-level coding down 20%, call centers down 15%
stories.summary: Stanford's top economists just shared actual data (not projections) showing AI is already hitting specific sectors hard. Entry-level software dev jobs for workers 22-26 dropped 20%, and call center hiring fell 15%, but new AI-adjacent roles are growing.
stories.takeaway: If you're in an AI-exposed field, you gotta adapt or risk getting left behind. The winners are those actively using AI to learn new skills, not just automating old ones.

stories.title: Tech Hub Pulse: South Florida positioning for massive AI and space tech investment surge
stories.summary: Hundreds of founders and investors gathered in Broward to discuss South Florida's tech future. Experts predict 3-5 major IPOs in 60 months and a huge investment wave in space/defense tech, with quantum computing already landing in Boca Raton.
stories.takeaway: South Florida is lowkey becoming a real tech hub, not just a crypto meme city. If you're in tech here, infrastructure growth and talent demand could be massive opportunities.

stories.title: Tech giants flexing AI budgets: Amazon $200B, Google $185B, Meta $135B — South Florida could benefit indirectly
stories.summary: The big three tech companies are dumping hundreds of billions into AI infrastructure. While most spending stays near existing hubs, experts say South Florida could see ripple effects through demand for skilled labor, engineering, construction, and data services.
stories.takeaway: The AI gold rush is real and South Florida's emerging tech corridor could catch some overflow. Skilled workers in engineering and data science might see more opportunities.

stories.title: Florida Chamber Foundation: AI transitioning from 'experiment' to 'essential business tool' in 2026
stories.summary: Healthcare, tourism, and manufacturing leaders all agreed that AI is fundamentally changing how work gets done across industries. Mayo Clinic expects AI to shift from experimental to operational in 2026, while manufacturers worry about workforce adaptation.
stories.takeaway: Tbh, every industry needs to figure out AI integration ASAP. The challenge isn't tech — it's training workers on how to actually use this stuff effectively.

watchNext.title: Routine white-collar jobs down 13%, analytical roles up 20% — Harvard Business School research
watchNext.why: Shows the bigger picture of AI job displacement. If you're doing repetitive work, fr you're at risk. But creative and analytical roles are actually growing.

watchNext.title: 450+ open IT and AI jobs in South Florida right now — salary data and requirements
watchNext.why: Lowkey solid resources to see what's actually hiring. Glassdoor, LinkedIn, and Indeed have hundreds of open positions in the region if you're job hunting.
